item.title: US hosts critical minerals event in Brazil amid diplomatic strains
item.description: The U.S. embassy will sponsor an event in Sao Paulo between U.S. investors and Brazilian firms looking to produce critical minerals on Wednesday amid diplomatic tensions that threaten to overshadow talks ​to firm up Western Hemisphere supply chains.

item.title: Disney's new CEO Josh D'Amaro steps up for a wild ride
item.description: Josh D'Amaro officially assumes his new role as Disney's chief executive officer at Wednesday's annual shareholder meeting, taking the helm of the entertainment colossus at a time of profound change.

item.title: US oil loan from emergency reserve depends on stiff premiums
item.description: A U.S. plan to help control global oil prices with a swap of millions of barrels of oil from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve depends on energy companies returning oil back to the facility with ​high premiums in the form of additional barrels, which some traders said could deter participation.

item.title: Is coffee the new cocoa? Some expect coffee prices to also crash
item.description: Some coffee industry experts have been drawing comparisons between the ​coffee and cocoa markets, projecting that coffee prices will sink in coming months, just as cocoa prices crashed after the chocolate-making ‌ingredient hit an all-time high in 2024.

item.title: Wall Street's bank capital victory in sight but hurdles remain
item.description: Wall Street banks are set to score a win as President Donald Trump's regulators unveil ​softened new draft capital rules this week, but there could still be fresh technical and political challenges ahead for the contentious project.

item.title: US drivers face long-term pain at pump, analysts say; Trump bets they are wrong
item.description: President Donald Trump and congressional Republicans are betting the oil-price shock sparked by the Iran crisis will be too short-lived to hurt them politically in November, but traders and ​industry analysts see signs that U.S. pump prices will stay painfully high long after any diplomatic breakthrough.

item.title: Fed to signal rate hike an option? Possible, though unlikely
item.description: Rising concern about higher inflation from surging oil prices is not going to press the Federal Reserve into an interest rate hike this week, but the idea could still find its way to the surface at a policy meeting overshadowed by the start of ​the Iran conflict just over two weeks ago.

item.title: Wall St futures gain as oil prices slip, focus on Fed outlook
item.description: U.S. stock index futures rose on Wednesday, buoyed by a modest drop in oil prices, while jittery ​investors awaited an update on the monetary policy and the economy from the Federal Reserve to assess the impact ‌of the Middle East conflict.

item.title: UniCredit CEO says positive discussions may lead to improved terms for Commerzbank bid
item.description: UniCredit Chief Executive Andrea Orcel said ​on Wednesday there ‌was a "remote" possibility the Italian bank would improve the ​terms of its ​bid for Commerzbank based ⁠on the outcome of ​their discussions.

item.title: Hong Kong IPO pipeline set to suffer amid Beijing's scrutiny of 'red-chip' listings
item.description: Beijing's tightened scrutiny of plans by Chinese companies incorporated outside mainland China to list in Hong Kong ​could have a significant impact on the city's rich IPO pipeline - at least in the short-term, bankers and lawyers said.

item.title: KKR to invest up to $310 million in India e-bus deal
item.description: U.S. private equity firm KKR will invest up to $310 million through a partnership with Indian electric ​commercial vehicle maker PMI Electro Mobility Solutions and ‌its e-bus platform Allfleet India, the companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: Tencent's quarterly revenue rises 13% on gaming, AI demand
item.description: Tencent Holdings reported a 13% increase in fourth-quarter revenue on Wednesday, driven by strong ​demand for gaming and growth in its artificial intelligence services, cementing ‌its position as China's largest social media and gaming company.

item.title: UniCredit CEO says has 'relatively constructive' approach to digital euro
item.description: The introduction of the digital ​euro will have ‌a negative impact on banks' liquidity and ​profit margins by ​taking away a portion ⁠of retail deposits, ​which are the cheapest ​source of funding, UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel said.

item.title: Samsung Elec and AMD sign MoU on AI memory, explore foundry partnership
item.description: Samsung Electronics and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) signed a memorandum of understanding to expand their strategic partnership on memory chip ​supplies for artificial intelligence infrastructure, the companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: BHP's new CEO seeks to firm ties in a rapidly changing world
item.description: BHP's new CEO Brandon Craig takes the reins at the world's largest listed miner as competition heats up for copper, geopolitical tensions escalate and relations with top customer China threaten to disrupt its strength in iron ore.

item.title: Alibaba's AI strategy shift comes into focus with big bets on agents
item.description: Alibaba is sharpening its artificial intelligence strategy by focusing on agents that connect the many businesses under its sprawling corporate umbrella.

item.title: Exail's order intake grows 87% propelled by underwater drone technology
item.description: French underwater drone maker Exail Technologies said on Wednesday its full-year ​current core profit grew ​40% to 103 million euros ($119 ⁠million), with order intake ​surging 87% to 844 million euros.

item.title: Gasoline price hike from Iran war could push consumers toward EVs, hybrids
item.description: The rising price of gasoline from the Iran war has triggered angst and uncertainty for carmakers, dealers and vehicle owners at the pump. For Martin Miller, it presents an opportunity.

item.title: Exclusive: Meta vowed to stop illegal financial ads in Britain. It failed 1,000 times in a week
item.description: U.S. tech giant Meta has repeatedly failed to stop illegal ads for high-risk financial products running on its platforms in Britain, despite committing to block them, according to a review by the country's financial regulator.

item.title: Samsung's unionised workers in South Korea approve strike plan
item.description: Samsung Electronics' unionised workers in South Korea voted on Wednesday to authorise a strike, deepening a labour dispute ​over bonuses and raising the risk of production disruptions at the world's ‌biggest memory chipmaker.

item.title: To dot, or not to dot, that is the question
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Stella Qiu

item.title: Nippon Steel secures $5.7 billion of loans for U.S. Steel takeover
item.description: Nippon Steel has ​secured loans totalling ‌900 billion yen ($5.67 billion) from the ​Japan Bank ​for International Cooperation (JBIC) ⁠and major Japanese ​private lenders ​for its acquisition of U.S. Steel, it ​said in ​a statement on Wednesday.

item.title: Microsoft weighs legal action over $50 billion Amazon-OpenAI cloud deal, FT reports
item.description: Microsoft is ​weighing legal action against ‌Amazon and OpenAI over a $50 billion deal that ​could breach Microsoft's exclusive ​cloud partnership with the ⁠ChatGPT maker, the Financial ​Times reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Fed likely to hold rates steady as Iran war shocks policy debate
item.description: Federal Reserve officials, convening in a wartime setting that began less than three weeks ago, are expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday, but more significantly to outline in a new policy statement and projections how they ​feel President Donald Trump's decision to launch an open-ended conflict with Iran has recast the outlook for the U.S. economy, inflation and monetary policy.

item.title: Chipmakers in Malaysia monitoring risks from helium supply disruptions, association says
item.description: Semiconductor firms in Malaysia are monitoring risks from disruptions to helium supplies due to the conflict in the Middle ​East, though the situation has not caused any operational interruptions so ‌far, an industry executive told Reuters.

item.title: A mystery AI model has developers buzzing: Is this DeepSeek's latest blockbuster?
item.description: A powerful artificial intelligence model that appeared anonymously on a developer platform last week has sparked speculation that Chinese startup DeepSeek may be quietly testing its next-generation system ahead of an official launch.

item.title: Samsung Elec plans to produce Tesla chips starting late 2027
item.description: Samsung Electronics ​said on Wednesday ‌that it expects to ​start ​volume production of Tesla's ⁠chips ​at Samsung's factory ​in Texas in the second ​half of ​next year.

item.title: Asian stocks rally as oil retreats, Fed in spotlight
item.description: Asian shares rallied on Wednesday as oil prices paused their gains, with markets turning to the U.S. Federal Reserve meeting to see ​how policymakers will balance growth and inflation risks amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Elliott Management confirms stake in Japan shipper Mitsui OSK
item.description: Elliott ​Investment Management ‌said on ​Wednesday ​that it ⁠has ​a built ​a "significant" investment in ​Japanese ​shipping company ‌Mitsui ⁠OSK Lines , confirming ​an ​earlier Reuters ⁠report.

item.title: Chinese authorities approve Nvidia's H200 AI chip sales, source says
item.description: Chinese authorities have granted approval for multiple Chinese companies ​to purchase H200 AI chips from Nvidia, ‌according to a person familiar with the situation.
